DESCRIPTION: The Bidding Law establishes guidelines for public procurement, guaranteeing equality, legality, and efficiency in the use of resources.
FUNDAMENTAL PRINCIPLES: Publicity, morality, legality, impartiality, efficiency, and economy.
TYPES OF BIDDING: Open bidding, price quotation, invitation to bid, electronic auction, and public auction.
PHASES OF THE BIDDING PROCESS: Preliminary technical study, bidding document, qualification, judgment, approval, and contracting.
JUDGMENT CRITERIA: Best technique, lowest price, highest discount, and highest economic return.
SANCTIONS AND PENALTIES: Suspension, fine, prohibition from bidding, and declaration of ineligibility.
